stories.title: Nelly Korda Wins US Women's Open — Fourth Major, Clutch Finish at Riviera
stories.angle: Korda birdies par-5 17th to take solo lead then makes wobbling par putt on 18 to win by 1; 8-under 276, final-round 69; Hull and Lopez tied for T2 at 7-under; Korda's 4th major and 2nd of 2026 (also won Chevron); $2.5 million prize; Hull led briefly with eagle at 1 but bogeyed 12 and 14

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda wins the 2026 U.S. Women's Open.

Birdie on 17 to take the lead. Wobbling par putt on 18 that barely fell in.

8-under 276. Hull and Lopez one back at T2.

Four majors. Two this season. What a player. #USWO #LPGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Charley Hull was SO close.

Eagle on 1. Led the US Women's Open late on Sunday.

Then bogeys on 12 and 14.

Korda wins. Hull tied for 2nd. One shot. One round that will hurt for a long time. #USWO #LPGATour

article.headline: Four Majors. Two This Season. Nelly Korda Wins the 2026 U.S. Women's Open at Riviera.

research.facts:
Nelly Korda Wins US Women's Open
**Sources:**
- NBC Sports: "U.S Women's Open 2026: Nelly Korda claims first ever championship win"
- Golf Channel: "Nelly Korda wins U.S. Women's Open with late birdie Sunday at Riviera"
- ESPN: "Nelly Korda wins the US Women's Open as last putt just falls in"
- Sky Sports: "US Women's Open: Nelly Korda claims thrilling victory as Charley Hull narrowly misses"
- Yahoo Sports: "Nelly Korda wins U.S. Women's Open at Riviera, fourth major championship title"
- TheGolfNewsNet: "2026 US Women's Open final results: Prize money payout"
**Key verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Korda won the 81st U.S. Women's Open [HIGH]
- Final score: 8-under 276, final round 2-under 69 [HIGH]
- Won by 1 shot over Charley Hull and Gaby Lopez (both 7-under 277) [HIGH]
- Korda birdied the par-5 17th with a 9-foot putt to take solo lead [HIGH]
- Korda made a par putt on 18 that "wobbled" but fell in [HIGH — ESPN: "last putt just falls in"]
- Prize: $2.5 million [HIGH]
- Fourth career major for Korda [HIGH]
- Second major of 2026 (also won Chevron Championship) [HIGH]
- Korda's first U.S. Women's Open win [HIGH]
- Venue: Riviera Country Club [HIGH]
- Charley Hull: eagle at par-5 1st, birdie 3, birdie 10, birdie 11 (briefly held outright lead), bogey 12, bogey 14, birdie 17, clutch par save at 18 [HIGH — Sky Sports/Golf Channel]
- In Gee Chun finished 4th; Sei Young Kim 5th [HIGH]

stories.title: JT Poston Wins Memorial in 2-Hole Playoff — 33-Hour Marathon Weekend
stories.angle: Poston beats Ryan Gerard on 2nd playoff hole after 33-hole Sunday marathon (R3 resumed + R4 played same day); Poston 12-under 276, birdie on 18 to force playoff; Clark 11-under solo 3rd; $4 million prize; 4th career PGA Tour win; punched ticket to US Open and next three majors; weather-delayed tournament stretched over multiple days

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
JT Poston at the Memorial this weekend:

Sunday morning: 4-shot lead
Sunday afternoon: lead gone, tied with Gerard
Regulation: 7-foot birdie on 18 to force playoff
Playoff: Gerard misses 6-footer, Poston wins

33 total holes on Sunday. $4 million. #PGATour #Memorial

article.headline: JT Poston Outlasts Gerard in 33-Hole Memorial Marathon to Win His Biggest Career Title

research.facts:
JT Poston Wins Memorial Tournament
**Sources:**
- Golf Channel: "J.T. Poston edges Ryan Gerard in Memorial Tournament playoff"
- ESPN: "J.T. Poston tops Ryan Gerard in playoff to capture Memorial"
- CBS Sports: "Memorial Tournament leaderboard 2026: J.T. Poston outlasts Ryan Gerard in two-hole playoff for fourth win"
- Yahoo Sports: "J.T. Poston wins the Memorial Tournament, punches ticket to U.S. Open after marathon round"
- Golf Digest: "How J.T. Poston went from zero top-20s this season to earning the biggest win of his career"
**Key verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Poston won the 2026 Memorial Tournament [HIGH]
- Poston and Gerard both finished at 12-under 276 [HIGH]
- Poston won on the second extra playoff hole (both holes were 18) [HIGH]
- Poston made a 7-foot birdie on 18 to force playoff in regulation [HIGH]
- Gerard made a 40-foot birdie on 17 to briefly lead, closed with 68 [HIGH]
- Gerard missed a 6-foot par putt on the second playoff hole to give Poston the win [HIGH]
- Total: 33 holes on Sunday (13 to complete R3 + full R4 + 2 playoff holes) [HIGH]
- Wyndham Clark finished solo 3rd at 11-under [HIGH]
- Sam Burns and Tommy Fleetwood finished two back [HIGH]
- Prize: $4 million [HIGH]
- Fourth PGA Tour win for Poston [HIGH]
- Won a spot in the next three majors (including US Open) [HIGH]
- Tournament: Memorial Tournament presented by Workday, Muirfield Village Golf Club [HIGH]

stories.title: Hatton Wins LIV Golf Valderrama — New Father Fends Off Rahm for Team Title
stories.angle: Tyrrell Hatton wins LIV Andalucia at 11-under 273, beating teammate Jon Rahm by 2 shots (Rahm closed with 67); Ancer T3 at 8-under; Garcia T4 at 7-under; Legion XIII wins team competition; Hatton recently became a father and referenced it post-round; $4M winner's share of $20M purse

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Tyrrell Hatton wins LIV Golf Andalucia at Valderrama.

11-under 273. Beats teammate Jon Rahm by 2 shots.

Rahm shot 67 in the final round — at his home course, in his home region of Spain.

His own teammate beat him. Legion XIII wins the team event too. #LIVGolf

article.headline: New Father Tyrrell Hatton Wins LIV Golf Andalucia — Beats Teammate Rahm at Valderrama

stories.title: US Open Preview — Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid, McIlroy's Redemption, Shinnecock Hills
stories.angle: 126th US Open June 18-21 at Shinnecock Hills, Southampton NY; Scheffler's grand slam bid (has Masters x2 + PGA + Open Championship); Scheffler's final round falls on his 30th birthday AND Father's Day; McIlroy arrives as reigning Masters champion; 10,201 entries; par 70/7,434 yards; Scheffler and McIlroy both scouted course — wide fairways (48 yards avg), extremely difficult greens

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
US Open. June 18-21. Shinnecock Hills.

Scottie Scheffler: Career Grand Slam on the line. Final round on his 30th birthday AND Father's Day.

Rory McIlroy: Reigning Masters champion. Never more dangerous.

10 days away. #USOpen #PGATour

research.facts:
US Open Preview
**Sources:**
- USGA: "Fast Facts For 2026 U.S. Open"
- Golf Digest: "U.S. Open 2026: Here's every player in the field at Shinnecock Hills"
- Washington Post / NBC Sports: Scheffler and McIlroy scouting reports on Shinnecock
- FanDuel Research: US Open Golf Odds for 2026
- Various: Scheffler grand slam context
**Key verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- 126th US Open at Shinnecock Hills Golf Club, Southampton, NY [HIGH]
- Dates: June 18-21, 2026 [HIGH]
- Course: par 70, 7,434 yards [HIGH]
- 10,201 entries (nearly all-time record) [HIGH]
- Defending champion: J.J. Spaun [HIGH]
- Scheffler's final round falls on his 30th birthday AND Father's Day [HIGH]
- Scheffler would complete career Grand Slam with a US Open win [HIGH — needs US Open; has Masters x2, PGA Championship, Open Championship]
- Fairways average 48 yards wide in 2026 [HIGH — Washington Post]
- McIlroy arrives as reigning Masters champion [HIGH]
- Shinnecock hosted previous US Opens in 1896, 1986, 1995, 2004, 2018 [HIGH]
- Koepka won there in 2018 [HIGH]

stories.title: The Weekend's Big Picture — Three Majors in Three Days, US Open 10 Days Away
stories.angle: Remarkable weekend: Korda claims USWO, Poston wins Memorial marathon, Hatton wins LIV Valderrama — three major results in one weekend; US Open begins June 18 (10 days away); LPGA and PGA Tour both produced dramatic finishes; golf's biggest week of June is just around the corner

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Golf this weekend:

🏆 Korda wins US Women's Open (4th major)
🏆 Poston wins Memorial in 2-hole playoff
🏆 Hatton wins LIV Valderrama

Three winners. Three tournaments. And the US Open starts in 10 days.

Great time to be a golf fan. #Golf #PGATour #LIVGolf #LPGATour

article.headline: Three Champions, One Weekend: Golf's Best Days Before the U.S. Open
